old_claim.note: Selective Seed Saving provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The selective-breeding component of the British Agricultural Revolution is livestock breeding, so the animal-breeding node is the direct prerequisite rather than ancient seed saving.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica describes the Agricultural Revolution as including selective breeding as part of the 18th-century British transformation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source ties the Agricultural Revolution node to selective breeding rather than to ancient crop seed-saving practice.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The replacement prevents laundering a plant-domestication seed-saving node into a livestock-improvement claim; the existing selective_animal_breeding node matches the source-backed selective-breeding component.

ontology_before: Agricultural Revolution & Enclosures depended on selective_seed_saving, conflating ancient crop domestication practice with early-modern livestock improvement.
ontology_after: Agricultural Revolution & Enclosures depends on selective_animal_breeding for the source-backed selective-breeding component.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: agricultural_revolution_enclosures now depends on selective_animal_breeding and no longer depends on selective_seed_saving.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to crop-variety selection rather than the British Agricultural Revolution mix of enclosure, rotation, and livestock breeding.
A source shows the intended selective-breeding edge is plant seed-saving rather than animal breeding.
The selective_animal_breeding node is narrowed away from choosing animals for meat, wool, milk, strength, speed, or other farm traits.
short_reason: Selective livestock breeding is the source-backed Agricultural Revolution prerequisite.
